title.en: Vehicle Ownership Transfer (RC Transfer) — Forms 29/30, Fees and Online Process

answerBox.en: To transfer a vehicle's RC after sale: seller and buyer sign Forms 29 and 30, the buyer applies to the RTO within 14 days (30 days for inter-state sales) with the RC, insurance, PUC and address proof, and pays a modest fee (₹150–500 for most vehicles plus smart-card charges). Most states now take the application online on parivahan.gov.in. Insurance must be transferred within 14 days too.

en.label: Insurance transfer
en.startsFrom: policy follows the vehicle, buyer must endorse

en.title: Paperwork on the day of sale
en.summary: Forms 29/30, delivery note, ID exchange — both sides keep copies.
en.detail: Execute **Form 29** (notice of transfer, signed by both) and **Form 30** (report of transfer) — download from [parivahan.gov.in](https://parivahan.gov.in). Add a **sale/delivery note** recording price, odometer, date-time of handover, and exchange ID/address proofs. The seller keeps signed copies: if the buyer never completes the transfer, these papers are the seller's shield (see our [sold-but-not-transferred guide](/help/vehicle-accident/vehicle-sold-not-transferred)).

en.title: Apply on Parivahan or at the RTO
en.summary: Online application, fee payment, then RTO verification.
en.detail: On **parivahan.gov.in → Vehicle Related Services**, choose "Transfer of Ownership", fill the application, upload documents (RC, insurance, PUC, buyer's address proof, Forms 29/30) and pay the fee — roughly **₹150–300 for two-wheelers, ₹300–500 for cars** plus smart-card charges; states vary. Book the RTO slot where biometric/document verification is required. **Hypothecated vehicles** need the financier's NOC (Form 35 closure first if the loan is done).

en.title: Clear the flags that block transfers
en.summary: Pending challans, tax dues and hypothecation stall RCs.
en.detail: Transfers stick on: **pending e-challans** (clear them first — our [challan guide](/help/police-criminal/traffic-challan-disputes)), road-tax arrears, an open **hypothecation** entry, or mismatch between the seller's RC address and records. Inter-state purchases additionally need an **NOC (Form 28)** from the origin RTO and re-registration in the new state. Fix these before applying; rejected applications forfeit time, not fees.

en.title: Close the loop: insurance, records, both parties
en.summary: Endorse the policy, verify the new RC, keep the trail.
en.detail: The buyer must get the **insurance policy endorsed** in their name within 14 days (third-party cover transfers automatically for that window; own-damage needs endorsement). Verify the new RC details on the [VAHAN portal](https://vahan.parivahan.gov.in) once issued. Sellers: confirm the transfer completed on VAHAN — until the RC changes, challans and civil liability chase the registered owner. Our ₹199 documentation service prepares the complete transfer set including the seller-protection affidavit.

rows.label: RTO transfer fee
rows.note: By vehicle class; plus smart-card charges

en.footnote: Never hand over a vehicle on a blank-signed RC or "we'll transfer later" promise — the registered owner remains liable for everything the vehicle does until the RC actually changes.

en.q: Who is responsible for the RC transfer — buyer or seller?
en.a: The buyer files the transfer application (Form 30 within 14 days), but the seller has a parallel duty to report the sale (Form 29) and every incentive to see it through — the registered owner stays liable for challans, taxes and accident claims until the RC changes. Both should track it to completion on VAHAN.

en.q: Can the transfer be done fully online?
en.a: In most states the application, fee and document upload are online on parivahan.gov.in, with one RTO visit for verification where required; several states are fully faceless for transfers. Aadhaar-authenticated services skip the visit entirely where enabled.

en.q: What if the vehicle still has a loan (hypothecation)?
en.a: The RC carries the financier's name; transfer needs either loan closure + Form 35 (hypothecation termination) with the bank NOC, or the financier's consent to transfer with the loan (rare). Close the hypothecation first — it is the most common reason applications bounce.

en.q: The seller/buyer is in another city and can't come to the RTO. Options?
en.a: Forms 29/30 can be signed and couriered — physical presence of both parties is generally not required if documents are complete and attested. For inter-state moves, the origin-RTO NOC (Form 28) can also be applied online. A specific-purpose power of attorney handles truly absent parties.

en.q: How do I check if the transfer actually happened?
en.a: Search the vehicle number on vahan.parivahan.gov.in — the owner name, hypothecation and fitness details are public. Sellers should screenshot the changed entry; that record is what ends your liability for the vehicle's future challans and accidents.

reviewer: Adv. Arjun Nair, Bar Council of Kerala, KER/0973/2012
author: WakilBhai Editorial Team
